titlestmt.titleproper: WHCU Department of Black Affairs records4904
titlestmt.author: Compiled by M.E. Warren

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-01-09 18:31:56 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: WHCU Department of Black Affairs records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: WHCU (Radio station : Ithaca, N.Y.). Department of Black Affairs

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: WHCU (Radio station : Ithaca, N.Y.). Department of Black Affairs

did.abstract: Tape recordings and scripts of the Night Sounds program broadcast on radio station WHCU in Ithaca, New York; interviews, promotional advertisements for local businesses, local news broadcasts, and other programs for the African American listening audience in Central New York State.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Tape recordings and scripts of the Night Sounds program broadcast on radio station WHCU in Ithaca, New York from 1974-1985. Includes promotional advertisements for local businesses; news broadcasts; programs for the African American listening audience in New York State, including African folktales for children and political issues; correspondence and fan mail; business files.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: WHCU Department of Black Affairs records, #4904. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collection 6580 was merged into this collection (March 2021).

controlaccess.subject: African Americans -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.
Radio stations -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.
Afro-American radio stations -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.
Afro-American mass media -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Recordings were reformatted onto Audio Cassettes in ~1991. These audio cassetes are now held by Cornell's Africana Library as their Night Sounds collection. This series was re-described as part of digitization of material in 2021. Some reels have had multiple identifiers over time and holding location. These identifiers are added as processing notes to the item record to cross-reference old numbers.

did.unittitle: Displaced Homemakers Committee|Tellin' it like it was week of 1/2/84

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interview with makers of the Displaced Homemaker Committee on the Job Readiness Program.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 36 and RMC NS#: 1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Forman gives an over view of SNCC, views on women workers, Black Panther Party and the Civil Rights Movemant of the 60's.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 29 and RMC NS#: 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Examines an approach to the subject of injustice and the black population by a black activist.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 31 and RMC NS#: 3

did.unittitle: African folktales: the story of the twins. Music: Nubian Sundance

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 26 and RMC NS#: 4

did.unittitle: American Justice: Interview with NY Assemblyman Arthur Eve discussing situation at Attica

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 28 and RMC NS#: 5

did.unittitle: Felipe Luciano: Backgrounds of Afro/Latino Sounds

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interview with Brother Felipe- History of Afro-Latino Music. He gives backgrounds on some of the most political Latino musicians. He talks about the relationship between African and Latin music. Includes some music sample of Cuban, Puerto Rican, Dominican, and other Hispanic music.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 48 and RMC NS#: 6

did.unittitle: Puerto Rico, Puerto Ricans, and American Justice

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interview with Armando Gonzales of La Association Del Caribe: Produce by Stan Ricks

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 30 and RMC NS#: 7

did.unittitle: Interview with Noel Mukono and Rest of the News.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interview with Noel Mukono, Secretary of Foreign Affairs of Zimbabwe African National Union (Zimbabwe, Rhodesia), who talks about the politics and government, social and health conditions of Zimbabwe. Rest of the News segment covers Ku Klux Klan killings; South Africa news brief; Local news

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 32 and RMC NS#: 8

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interview with James Turner and Robert Staples about studies on Black families, 10/22

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 35 and RMC NS#: 9

did.unittitle: Dimension series: Black students at Ithaca High School.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1. Desdemona Jacobs, 2. Sam Boone, 3. Steve Bryant, 4. Marjory Penalver, 5. Stephanie Orduna, 6. Mary McGinnis, 7. IHS Students

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 33 and RMC NS#: 10

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Major obligations of African Americans who are interested in the liberation movement.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 34 and RMC NS#: 11

did.unittitle: Africa is calling program tape 1: The Oriental Bros. Muri Tala Album|Tellin' it like it was week of 1983-11-28

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From the L.P. Muri Tala (We are the kings of Africa). The Oriental Brothers are Cameronese living at the time of this recording in eastern Nigeria. Recorded in both Igbo and English.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 26 and RMC NS#: 12

did.unittitle: The Native American Struggle: Interviews with Coyote and Anna of White Roots of Peace

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interviews with Coyote and Ann of White Roots of Peace by Steve Reaves. Also present at the interview was Toretha Scott, Ithaca News Times by Nightsound (Cornell U.)s WHCU-FM Ithaca, NY

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 24 and RMC NS#: 13

did.unittitle: Farewell Reception for Lynn M. Harvey, Director at WHCU-Nightsounds from 1981-1984

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Farewell Reception for Lynn M. Harvey; Reception held at the Elks Nest-536 West Green Street in Ithaca. Lynn was Asst. Director at WHCU-Nightsound (Cornell U.)s from 1981-1984. Speakers; 1. Annette Laurier-Skates 2. Larry Watson 3. June Wall Williams 4. Michelle Dade 5. Lynn Harvey.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 29 and RMC NS#: 14

did.unittitle: Reel 3: White Roots|David Sebiko, Pan-African Congress of Azainia (South Africa)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Detailed Interview with Sebiko- Just returning from appearing before the security council of the United Nations. Outlines of the Azania liberation movements agenda. Speaks on how the Western Media have undermined the anti-apartheid movement and have supported the minority government. He also points out the role that the United States government played in helping the minority regime stay in power.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 8 and RMC NS#: 15

did.unittitle: Black History Month 1984, week 1: Ancient African Queens with Cynthia Nwizu, plus "Street Beat" with Ken Glover (9 individual interviews)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: History of ancient African Queens with Cynthia Nwizu; Black Womanhood in celebration of Black History Month-Understanding the importance of women in the Black community and the family. Focus on her strength and spirit. Overview of some former African Queens; 1. Hatshepsut (5:27 min.) Ruled Ancient Egypt during the 17th dynasty. 2. Queen of Sheba-Makeda, ruler of Ethiopia. 3. Cleopatra-Queen of Egypt, became a goddess. 4. Queen of Zehobia, biographical stetch. 5. Queen Nzinga of Southern Africa.–Street Beat with Ken Glover, individual interviews.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 27 and RMC NS#: 16

did.unittitle: Interview with Bobby Byrd of Black Ivory

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: History of Band and the musicians background and how the Black Ivory band was formed in the year 1969

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 44 and RMC NS#: 17

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: RMC NS#: 18

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Explanation of the Summer Academy program in NYC. This program was designed to expose inner city younsters who might not have had the opportunity to be exposed to college. Dr. Abdulmalik gave some history on the program and explained the importance of keeping this program alive. The main focus is about ways to encourage and to give high school students the assistance they need to get a higher education.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 39 and RMC NS#: 19

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Discussion on the problems in Dominica. Sandy Black host; George Nelson-guest. Nelson was a graduate student at Cornell who was a native Dominican. He spoke of the struggles that were going on in Dominica and how the people were organizing to deal with overcoming those problems.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 17 and RMC NS#: 20

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bubba Brown's comments on COSEP.

did.unittitle: Prof. Murapa interview - the next Vietnam

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: RMC NS#: 31

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: RMC NS#: 32

did.unittitle: The Seven Days of Kwanzaa by Lynn M. Harvey

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hosted by Lynn M. Harvey. The history of what Kwanzaa is and means within the African American community. Kwanzaa is a holiday of unity for African people. Music throughout tape.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 28 and RMC NS#: 33

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Demonstration against apartheid in South Africa: Interview focus on the anti-apartheid movement in Ithaca. With an opinion offered on how people can rally and hold government officials accountable for their efforts and actions to help end apartheid in South Africa.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 23 and RMC NS#: 34

did.unittitle: Part of a conversation with Michael Haney, a Seminole from the National Office of the American Indian Movement, taken on Nov. 1 at Eagle Bay, N.Y. (Indian Encampment)

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 14 and RMC NS#: 35

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 14 and RMC NS#: 35

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Explanation of the Second Principle of Kwanzaa by Ken Glover; Kujichagulia, which stands for self-determination.

processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 16 and RMC NS#: 36

did.unittitle: Silver Hill Recording week of Nov. 12, 1979

processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: RMC NS#: 37

processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: RMC NS#: 38

did.unittitle: Ojeda Penn, "South Africa," from the LP All is One

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Political song about what is going on in South Africa from the LP All is One.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 20 and RMC NS#: 39

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Biography sketch on Malcolm X. Looks at Malcolm X's early childhood. Deals with the early influences in his life, which included his father and the UNIA (Universal Negro Improvement Association). After Malcolm's father was killed, his mother was placed in a mental hospital. Once Malcolm reached early adulthood he lived with his sister at Boston and turned into a life of crime. He eventually landed in prison where the teachings of Elijah Muhammad and the Nation of Islam were introduced to him. upon leaving prison Malcolm joined the Nation and became its spokes person.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 5 and RMC NS#: 40

did.unittitle: Ebony Insight: Affirmative action - what's up?, out: Truely[sic] feel|Black amateur poetry #1: D. Alexander, intro: music fades

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: RMC NS#: 41

did.unittitle: Leslie Gross: Ebony Insight: Black Solidarity Day|Leslie Gross: Ebony Insight: Atlanta murders; Emery King speaks; Rhythm rendezvous promos

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Story about a questionable conviction for the murder of a Black Man in Atlanta, Georgia.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 21 and RMC NS#: 42

did.unittitle: Wanda Motley's State of Black America, Campaign 1980

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Anti- Blacks sentiments and how it affects Blacks and politics.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 12 and RMC NS#: 43

did.unittitle: News Control. Side 1: Ad Hoc student committee press release| Side 2: Statements: coalition and students for 4/6

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: RMC NS#: 44

did.unittitle: The State of Black America segment: Blacks in the military, part II with Fred Ennis

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Overview of Blacks role in American's battles. Focus on the wars against the Native Americans, Civil War, Spanish American War, World War One, World War Two, and the Vietnam War. There is also a look at the hypocrisy of American policy in which Jim Crow was enforced; Blacks fought and died in the name of America, but were still lynched once they returned to the United States. There is also discussion about the economic reasons why Blacks join the military in disproportionate numbers.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 33 and RMC NS#: 45

did.unittitle: Black World News with Linda Gadsby

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1. Student Protest in South Africa , seven journalists had been arrested 2. Violence in London — 10/ 2/85 Linda Gadsby– 10/2/85 1. Kappa's second food feast 2. College Women and men careers' before marriage 3. Black dying at higher rates than whites. Cancer, heart failures, substance abuse are among causes of death– homicide is leading cause of death for Black men between ages 15-44. Linda Gadsby — 10/30/85 1. House defeats cuts on food stamp bill.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 35 and RMC NS#: 46

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Biography sketch of Gillispie and overview of the Bop Era.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 31 and RMC NS#: 47

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With Ray Cooke Political Songs — Folk Songs dealing with the plight of the American Indians.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 34 and RMC NS#: 48

did.unittitle: Black World News with Arnold Stovell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1)Filipino General Marcos takes a leave–Oct. 15, 1985 2)Ebony insights–The Greying of Black music–Detailing the importance of music within the Black community. Blacks should be less concerned with trying to crossover in order to appeal to white audience. 3)Ebony insights–Blues for Black actors–Blacks are under represented in cinema. Most successful are comics e.g. Richard Pryor, Eddie Murphy. 4)Mondale retires, will return to D.C. and practice law. 5)State of Black America Report–1985–Blacks made progress in the 1960's; peaked in the 1970's; and have fallen off ever since. Wide gap between Black and Whites in earnings.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 47 and RMC NS#: 49

did.unittitle: Black World News with Ken Glover

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1)Earthquake in Mexico City–Sept. 21, 1985 2)Immigration bill–Sept. 20, 1985 3)Quake in Chile–Sept. 27, 1985 4)Israel will not place sanctions on South Africa, will keep ties with South Africa trading partners–Sept. 27, 1985 5)Voter fraud case in Alabama–Oct. 4, 1985 6)School Funds scandal in St. Louis–Oct. 25, 1985 7)Protest in Capetown, South Africa 8)Affirmative Action–Nov. 1, 1985 9)C. Scott King praises Gov. Cuomo for Passing of King holiday–Nov. 1, 1985 10)Jesse Jackson calls for boycott of ships doing business with South Africa–Nov. 8, 1985 11)State pay disparities between male/female/Black/White/ Latino–Nov. 8, 1985 12)Syracuse University will review plan in divestment issue with South Africa–Nov. 15, 1985

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 47 and RMC NS#: 50

did.unittitle: Black World News with Sonji Stevenson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1) Latin peace talks cancelled–Feb. 14, 1985 2) Shultz accuses Nicaragua of falling behind Iran curtain– Feb. 20, 1985 3) One hundred and forty eight are killed as Spanish Jet hits mountain (double-dub) Feb. 20, 1985 4) Ethiopian Jews forcefully removed to Israel–Feb. 27, 1985 5) Delegation of five bishops visit Nicaragua Feb.27, 1985 6) Summary of Goetz case–March 2, 1985 7) Israeli jets attack guerilla bases in Lebanon–11/25/84 8) Kenya President wants government to play his tune–11/3/84 9) Ethiopia gets new aid from U.S.–Oct. 26, 1984 10) Violation ruled out when restaurant owner refuses to serve Blacks–Dec. 1, 1984 11) Ethiopia guerilla release 3 hostages taken 6 weeks ago 12) Honduras to open a new U.S. base–Dec. 1, 1984 13) Human rights in Haiti–March 13,1984 14) Two congressmen held after South African embassy protest- -Dec. 1, 1984 15) Goetz case reopened–March 13, 1985 16) Brazil tries to stop cocaine traffic–March 14, 1985 17) New witness for Goetz case–March 16, 1985 18) Grenada reports Economic revival–March 27, 1985 19) Trinidad adjusts it's view on the outside world–4/3/85 20) South Africans testify on the use of heavy ammunition– April 10, 1985 21)Haitians removed from high risk category for AIDS–4/10/85 22)Judge dismissed indictment of Police Officer Steven Sullivan in Eleanor Bumpurs killing–April 17, 1985

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Alternative numbering: Africana audio cassette NS#: 8

did.unittitle: African Folk Tales: The Enchanted Buck

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: African Folk Tales-The Enchanted Buck. A young bride is accused of being a witch by her groom's father when the kill for her wedding feast turns into a white buck. The groom's father calls the wedding off and the bride goes back to the village where she sees the buck again. She follows the buck into a forest in which a hungry ogre grabs for the buck and when he does so, the buck turns into a handsome chief. The chief asks the young woman to be his bride. The moral of the story is that people should not always kill animals to eat for pleasure.
